title: rourea minor subsp. monadelpha
intro: Rourea minor subsp. monadelpha, also known as the Lesser Rourou, is a plant species native to New Caledonia and Vanuatu, belonging to the family Connaraceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory activities. The health benefits of Rourea minor subsp. monadelpha include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction, infusion, or ointment to harness its medicinal properties.
